params.name: samples
params.description: Return all sample data for this exercise.

params.name: zones
params.description: Return all zones data for this exercise.

params.name: route
params.description: Return all route data for this exercise.

items.docs:
List users exercises available in AccessLink. Only Exercises uploaded to Flow in the last 30 days are returned. Only exercises that have been uploaded to Flow after the user is registered with your client will be returned. Use samples and zones query parameters to return optional samples and zone information of the exercises.
Training Load Pro data will be included in the exercise object if the exercise is recorded with a specific wrist unit model,  please check [compatible devices](https://www.

params.name: samples
params.description: Return all sample data for this exercise.

params.name: zones
params.description: Return all zones data for this exercise.

params.name: route
params.description: Return all route data for this exercise.

items.docs: Get users exercise using hashed id. Only Exercises uploaded to Flow in the last 30 days are available. Only exercises that have been uploaded to Flow after the user is registered with your client will be available. Use samples and zones query parameters to return optional samples and zone information of the exercise.

FIT file for users exercise. Only Exercises uploaded to Flow in the last 30 days are available. Only exercises that have been uploaded to Flow after the user is registered with your client will be available. See [FIT sport mappings in appendix](#sport-type-mapping-in-fit-files).

TCX file for users exercise without an AccessLink "transaction". Only Exercises uploaded to Flow in the last 30 days are available. Only exercises that have been uploaded to Flow after the user is registered with your client will be available.

GPX file for users exercise without an AccessLink "transaction". Only Exercises uploaded to Flow in the last 30 days are available. Only exercises that have been uploaded to Flow after the user is registered with your client will be available.
Once partner has been authorized by user, partner must register the user before being able to access its data. API user-id and Polar User Id (polar-user-id) are interchangeable terms.

List user basic information.
Note: Although it is possible to get users weight and height from this resource, the [get physical info](#get-physical-info) should be used instead.
When partner wishes no longer to receive user data, user can be de-registered. This will revoke the access token authorized by user.
Retrieve user's physical information.
Get list of available exercises and activities for users

Check for new training data and create a new transaction if found. Only data uploaded to Flow in the last 30 days will be available. Only data that has been uploaded to Flow after the user is registered with your client will be available.
After successfully initiating a transaction, training sessions included within it can be retrieved with the provided transactionId.
After successfully retrieving training session data within a transaction, partners are expected to commit the transaction.
Retrieve training session summary data
Retrieve exercise in FIT format. See [FIT sport mappings in appendix](#sport-type-mapping-in-fit-files).

params.name: includePauseTimes
params.description: Whether to add pauses as part of the route. Default is false.

items.docs: Retrieve training session summary data in GPX format

Retrieve list of links to available samples in training session

Retrieve exercise in TCX format

Initiate activity transaction.
Check for new activity summaries and create a new transaction if found.
Only data uploaded to Flow in the last 30 days will be available.
Only data that has been uploaded to Flow after the user is registered with your client will be available.

List new activity data.
After successfully initiating a transaction, activity summaries included within it can be retrieved with the provided transactionId.
After successfully retrieving activity summary data within a transaction, partners are expected to commit the transaction.
Get activity summary
Get activity step samples. Example data can be seen from [appendix](#activity-step-time-series).
Get activity zone samples. Example data can be seen from [appendix](#activity-zone-time-series).

params.name: from
params.description: Start date in format YYYY-MM-DD. If not given, defaults to 28 days ago.

params.name: to
params.description: End date in format YYYY-MM-DD. If not given, defaults to today.

params.name: steps
params.description: include steps samples for activity summary.

params.name: activity_zones
params.description: include activity zones samples for activity summary.

params.name: inactivity_stamps
params.description: include inactivity stamps for activity summary.

items.docs: List users activities available in AccessLink. When no date range is given, activities from the last 28 days are returned. When `from` is given, activities for the specified date range are returned. From date cannot be older than 365 days from today and the maximum range between from and to is 28 days.

params.name: steps
params.description: include steps samples for activity summary.

params.name: activity_zones
params.description: include activity zones samples for activity summary.

params.name: inactivity_stamps
params.description: include inactivity stamps for activity summary.

items.docs: Get users activity for given date. Date cannot be older than 365 days from today.

params.name: from
params.description: Start date in format YYYY-MM-DD. If not given, defaults to 28 days ago.

params.name: to
params.description: End date in format YYYY-MM-DD. If not given, defaults to today.

items.docs: List users activity samples available in AccessLink. When no date range is given, activity samples from the last 28 days are returned. When `from` is given, activity samples for the specified date range are returned. From date cannot be older than 365 days from today and the maximum range between from and to is 28 days.

info.name: Get activity samples for given date

items.docs: Get users activity samples for given date. Date cannot be older than 365 days from today.

Initiate physical info transaction.
Check for new physical info and create a new transaction if found.

List new physical info data.
After successfully initiating a transaction, physical infos included within it can be retrieved with the provided transactionId.
After successfully retrieving physical information within a transaction, partners are expected to commit the transaction.
Get physical info entity data
Returns created webhook if exists.

Create new webhook.

When creating webhook the AccessLink sends a ping message to the url in request body. The ping message must be answered with 200 OK or otherwise the webhook is not created.

Created webhook is by default in activated state and will send push notifications about subscribed events. Webhook will be automatically deactivated if push notifications have not been successful for a period of seven (7) days.
**Note!** Save the *signature_secret_key* from response since this is the onl
Activate deactivated webhook. Activation is successful if the configured webhook url returns HTTP status 200 (OK) for the issued ping request.
Deactivate activated webhook. Deactivated webhook does not push notifications about subscribed events.

Edit webhook event types and/or url.

When updating webhook url the AccessLink sends a ping message to the new address. The ping message must be answered with 200 OK or otherwise the webhook is not updated.
Delete webhook by id.
List sleep data of user for the last 28 days.

params.name: date
params.description: Date of sleep as ISO-8601 date string, example: "2020-01-01"

items.docs: Get Users sleep data for given date.
Get the dates with sleep start and end times, where user has sleep data available in the last 28 days.

items.docs: Get users continuous heart rate values for given date range. Supported devices

Returns cardio load data for the last 28 days. The response list contains cardio load objects for every day in the range, even if values cannot be calculated — in that case the cardio load status is represented as LOAD_STATUS_NOT_AVAILABLE.

params.name: date
params.description: Date as ISO-8601 date string, example: "2022-01-01"

items.docs: Returns cardio load data for selected date.

info.name: Get cardio load by date range

params.name: from
params.description: Inclusive as ISO-8601 date string, example: "2022-01-01"

params.name: to
params.description: Inclusive as ISO-8601 date string, example: "2022-01-01"

items.docs: Returns cardio load data for selected date range. The response list contains cardio load objects for every day between range even if the cardio load values cannot be calculated, in this case the cardio load status is represented as LOAD_STATUS_NOT_AVAILABLE.
Returns historical cardio load data for selected period of days counting from current date.
Returns historical cardio load data for selected period of months counting from current date.
